items.summary: Add a one-time admin bootstrap mechanism to the backend: if `adminPrincipal` has never been set (i.e., it holds a sentinel/anonymous value from initial deployment), the first authenticated caller of a new `claimAdmin` update function is automatically and permanently granted the `adminPrincipal` role. Subsequent calls to `claimAdmin` must be rejected once an admin is already set. All existing admin-only functions continue to require the caller to match the stored `adminPrincipal`.

Expose a `claimAdmin` call from the frontend Admin Dashboard page: if the authenticated user is not currently admin and no admin has been set yet (backend returns a designated 'unclaimed' status), display a prominent 'Claim Admin' button. On click, call the `claimAdmin` backend function and refresh the admin status check. If an admin is already set and the current user is not that admin, continue showing the existing 'Access Denied' message.
